commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c4d0e879d646fc809f9f0345d8f6c2421a7181e1)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds source-wide memory/session embedding batching, OpenAI-compatible batch endpoint support, verbose source-wide batch logging, and focused memory/provider tests.

PR surface: Source +759, Tests +398, Other -2. Total +1155 across 24 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the remaining large-case blocker: the May 31 discussion shows a reporter run splitting at reason=max-chunks, and the PR source still flushes source-wide groups at 4096 chunks. I did not rerun the CLI locally in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blockers involve SDK contract, source-wide batching limits, and upgrade behavior decisions beyond a narrow mechanical repair.

target.title: perf(memory): batch memory embeddings across files
target.body:
## Summary

- Batch-enabled source-wide embedding now defers memory and session source indexing into one shared queue before submitting provider batches.
- OpenAI-compatible source-wide verbose logs now print the batching path, source distribution, file count, chunk count, group, and flush reason.
- Source-wide file batching now covers the reported large mixed memory+session case while the generic embedding provider SDK contract stays narrow.
- Regression coverage keeps forced memory+session indexing on one provider runtime batch and verifies the OpenAI-compatible remote batch path.

Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88009

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw memory index --force --verbose` should not submit separate OpenAI-compatible embedding batches for memory files and session files when both sources are enabled; the verbose output should identify the source-wide batching path and the source distribution.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux local OpenClaw CLI from `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-88238-ci-20260531`, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, 3 memory markdown files, 2 session transcript files, provider `openai`, model `test-embed`, batch enabled, local OpenAI-compatible batch HTTP endpoint on `127.0.0.1:18084`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-88238-ci-20260531
PORT=18084 STATS_PATH=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-88238-evidence/openai-compatible-batch-stats-current.json \
  node /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-88238-evidence/openai-compatible-batch-server.mjs &
server_pid=$!
OPENCLAW_HOME=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-88238-evidence/runtime-home \
NO_COLOR=1 \
pnpm openclaw memory index --force --verbose \
  > /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-88238-evidence/openclaw-memory-index-current.log 2>&1
kill "$server_pid"
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
Memory Index (main)
Sources: memory (MEMORY.md + $OPENCLAW_HOME/workspace/memory/*.md), sessions ($OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.jsonl)
[memory] embeddings: source-wide batch prepare files=5 sources=memory=3,sessions=2 maxFiles=2048 maxChunks=4096
[memory] embeddings: source-wide batch submit group=1 source=memory+sessions files=5 chunks=5 sources=memory=3,sessions=2 reason=end
[memory] embeddings: openai batch submit
[memory] embeddings: openai batch created
Memory index updated (main).
```

```json
{
  "uploads": [
    {
      "fileId": "file-1",
      "requests": 5,
      "inputs": [
        "# Log\nAlpha memory line.\nZebra memory line.\n",
        "# Log\nBeta memory line.\n",
        "# Log\nGamma memory line.\n",
        "User: Session alpha memory line.",
        "Assistant: Session beta memory line."
      ]
    }
  ],
  "batches": [
    {
      "batchId": "batch-1",
      "inputFileId": "file-1",
      "outputFileId": "output-1",
      "requests": 5
    }
  ],
  "statusPolls": [],
  "outputReads": [
    "output-1"
  ]
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** One OpenAI-compatible batch was created with 5 requests spanning both configured sources: 3 memory files and 2 session transcript files. The console output now makes the source-wide code path, source distribution, file count, chunk count, and larger file cap visible.
- **What was not tested:** A live third-party embedding endpoint was not tested because this environment has no embedding base URL/API key/model variables. The real OpenClaw CLI and OpenAI-compatible provider path were tested against a local OpenAI-compatible batch endpoint.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: focused runtime tests, source-wide memory integration test, lint, and type lanes.
- Target test file: `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`; `src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts`; `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.bridge.test.ts`; `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/batch-http.test.ts`; `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/post-json.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: forced memory+session indexing shares one source-wide provider runtime batch; OpenAI-compatible batch runtime uses upload/create/output endpoints; batch helper tests stay mocked at the HTTP seam without opening real network connections.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it covers the real source queue, provider runtime bridge, OpenAI-compatible batch transport, and the CI mock seam that regressed without widening unrelated runtime coverage.

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/batch-http.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/post-json.test.ts src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.bridge.test.ts --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 65.15s

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 57.60s

OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/batch-http.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/post-json.test.ts src/media-understanding/defaults.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.proxy.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.startup-retry.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.transcript-policy.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts src/agents/transcript-policy.policy.test.ts src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts src/commands/models/list.provider-catalog.test.ts src/commands/migrate/apply.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-auth.test.ts src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.helpers.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth.mirror-refresh.test.ts --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 5 Vitest shards in 87.36s

node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs
both completed with exit code 0

pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
pnpm tsgo:core
both completed with exit code 0

git diff --check
exit code 0
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The previous source-wide path still indexed memory files and session files through separate source sync calls. That made the OpenAI-compatible provider submit separate batches per source, and the verbose output did not expose enough source-wide batching details to distinguish that path from per-file or per-source batching.
- Missing detection / guardrail: The tests did not lock the forced memory+session path to a single provider runtime batch, and the generic batch helper tests were coupled to module initialization order that could accidentally open real network requests when shared imports changed.
target.author: mushuiyu886

```text
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/batch-http.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/post-json.test.ts src/media-understanding/defaults.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.proxy.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.startup-retry.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.transcript-policy.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts src/agents/transcript-policy.policy.test.ts src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts src/commands/models/list.provider-catalog.test.ts src/commands/migrate/apply.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-auth.test.ts src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.helpers.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth.mirror-refresh.test.ts --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 5 Vitest shards in 87.36s

node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs
both completed with exit code 0

pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
pnpm tsgo:core
both completed with exit code 0

git diff --check
exit code 0
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The previous source-wide path still indexed memory files and session files through separate source sync calls. That made the OpenAI-compatible provider submit separate batches per source, and the verbose output did not expose enough source-wide batching details to distinguish that path from per-file or per-source batching.
- Missing detection / guardrail: The tests did not lock the forced memory+session path to a single provider runtime batch, and the generic batch helper tests were coupled to module initialization order that could accidentally open real network requests when shared imports changed.

commands.author: mushuiyu886

target.title: perf(memory): batch memory embeddings across files
target.body:
## Summary

- Batch-enabled source-wide embedding now defers memory and session source indexing into one shared queue before submitting provider batches.
- OpenAI-compatible source-wide verbose logs now print the batching path, source distribution, file count, chunk count, group, and flush reason.
- Source-wide file batching now covers the reported large mixed memory+session case while the generic embedding provider SDK contract stays narrow.
- Regression coverage keeps forced memory+session indexing on one provider runtime batch and verifies the OpenAI-compatible remote batch path.

Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88009

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw memory index --force --verbose` should not submit separate OpenAI-compatible embedding batches for memory files and session files when both sources are enabled; the verbose output should identify the source-wide batching path and the source distribution.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux local OpenClaw CLI from `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-88238-ci-20260531`, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, 3 memory markdown files, 2 session transcript files, provider `openai`, model `test-embed`, batch enabled, local OpenAI-compatible batch HTTP endpoint on `127.0.0.1:18084`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-88238-ci-20260531
PORT=18084 STATS_PATH=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-88238-evidence/openai-compatible-batch-stats-current.json \
  node /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-88238-evidence/openai-compatible-batch-server.mjs &
server_pid=$!
OPENCLAW_HOME=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-88238-evidence/runtime-home \
NO_COLOR=1 \
pnpm openclaw memory index --force --verbose \
  > /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-88238-evidence/openclaw-memory-index-current.log 2>&1
kill "$server_pid"
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
Memory Index (main)
Sources: memory (MEMORY.md + $OPENCLAW_HOME/workspace/memory/*.md), sessions ($OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.jsonl)
[memory] embeddings: source-wide batch prepare files=5 sources=memory=3,sessions=2 maxFiles=2048 maxChunks=4096
[memory] embeddings: source-wide batch submit group=1 source=memory+sessions files=5 chunks=5 sources=memory=3,sessions=2 reason=end
[memory] embeddings: openai batch submit
[memory] embeddings: openai batch created
Memory index updated (main).
```

```json
{
  "uploads": [
    {
      "fileId": "file-1",
      "requests": 5,
      "inputs": [
        "# Log\nAlpha memory line.\nZebra memory line.\n",
        "# Log\nBeta memory line.\n",
        "# Log\nGamma memory line.\n",
        "User: Session alpha memory line.",
        "Assistant: Session beta memory line."
      ]
    }
  ],
  "batches": [
    {
      "batchId": "batch-1",
      "inputFileId": "file-1",
      "outputFileId": "output-1",
      "requests": 5
    }
  ],
  "statusPolls": [],
  "outputReads": [
    "output-1"
  ]
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** One OpenAI-compatible batch was created with 5 requests spanning both configured sources: 3 memory files and 2 session transcript files. The console output now makes the source-wide code path, source distribution, file count, chunk count, and larger file cap visible.
- **What was not tested:** A live third-party embedding endpoint was not tested because this environment has no embedding base URL/API key/model variables. The real OpenClaw CLI and OpenAI-compatible provider path were tested against a local OpenAI-compatible batch endpoint.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: focused runtime tests, source-wide memory integration test, lint, and type lanes.
- Target test file: `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`; `src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts`; `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.bridge.test.ts`; `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/batch-http.test.ts`; `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/post-json.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: forced memory+session indexing shares one source-wide provider runtime batch; OpenAI-compatible batch runtime uses upload/create/output endpoints; batch helper tests stay mocked at the HTTP seam without opening real network connections.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it covers the real source queue, provider runtime bridge, OpenAI-compatible batch transport, and the CI mock seam that regressed without widening unrelated runtime coverage.

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/batch-http.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/post-json.test.ts src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.bridge.test.ts --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 65.15s

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 57.60s

OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/batch-http.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/post-json.test.ts src/media-understanding/defaults.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.proxy.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.startup-retry.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.transcript-policy.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts src/agents/transcript-policy.policy.test.ts src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts src/commands/models/list.provider-catalog.test.ts src/commands/migrate/apply.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-auth.test.ts src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.helpers.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth.mirror-refresh.test.ts --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 5 Vitest shards in 87.36s

node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs
both completed with exit code 0

pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
pnpm tsgo:core
both completed with exit code 0

git diff --check
exit code 0
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The previous source-wide path still indexed memory files and session files through separate source sync calls. That made the OpenAI-compatible provider submit separate batches per source, and the verbose output did not expose enough source-wide batching details to distinguish that path from per-file or per-source batching.
- Missing detection / guardrail: The tests did not lock the forced memory+session path to a single provider runtime batch, and the generic batch helper tests were coupled to module initialization order that could accidentally open real network requests when shared imports changed.
